title: carex sylvatica subsp. sylvatica
intro: Carex sylvatica subsp. sylvatica, also known as woodland sedge, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Cyperaceae and is commonly found in deciduous and mixed woodlands in Europe. The medicinal properties of Carex sylvatica subsp. sylvatica include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions such as urinary tract infections and kidney stones. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular choice in herbal medicine. Carex sylvatica subsp. sylvatica can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin for therapeutic benefits.
